codeReview.summary: The project scaffold is functionally complete with Next.js 14 (App Router), FastAPI with modular domain structure, PostgreSQL/SQLAlchemy, Redis, MinIO, and Docker Compose orchestration. All services start and respond correctly. However, commit 07532e4 only contains .factory/ infrastructure files - the actual scaffold files (apps/web, apps/api/internal) are uncommitted changes. Additionally, orphaned Go files remain in the Python FastAPI codebase.

issues.description: Orphaned Go files present in Python FastAPI codebase: auth_adapter.go, server.go, handler_test.go. These are remnants from a previous Go-based scaffold attempt. While not blocking the current Python/FastAPI implementation, they create confusion and should be removed.
Orphaned Go files present alongside Python files: google.go, service.go, service_test.go, session.go, session_test.go. Same issue as above - remnants from Go scaffold.
config.go (Go file) present in Python project. Should be Python config.py only.

Commit 07532e4 only contains .factory/ infrastructure files (skills, services.yaml, library docs, init.sh). The actual project scaffold (apps/web with Next.js 14, apps/api/internal with FastAPI modules) exists as uncommitted changes in the working directory. The scaffold is functionally verified per handoff but not properly committed.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The transcript skeleton shows the worker followed the backend-worker skill procedure correctly. However, AGENTS.md at mission dir level (missionDir/AGENTS.md) was not found during transcript review - AGENTS.md was at project .factory/ level instead. This is not an error but worth noting for skill consistency.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows worker read .factory/services.yaml, .factory/init.sh, docker-compose.yml, apps directories. AGENTS.md location discrepancy may cause confusion for future workers.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The handoff reports successful docker compose up and verification via curl to ports 3100 and 3101. However, the commit (07532e4) does not contain the actual scaffold files. This suggests the scaffold was built before this commit and exists as uncommitted changes.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: git status shows uncommitted changes in apps/web and apps/api. commit 07532e4 only adds .factory/ files. docker compose verification would require actual app files to be present.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Droid-Shield is blocking git commit due to false positive detection of DATABASE_URL patterns in .env.example and config.py. The DATABASE_URL uses environment variable interpolation (${DB_USER}:${DB_PASSWORD}@...) which are placeholders, not actual secrets.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff discoveredIssues: 'Droid-Shield is blocking git commit due to false positive detection of database URL patterns in .env.example and config.py. These are placeholder values, not actual secrets.'

summary: The project-scaffold feature is functionally complete - Next.js 14 with App Router, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and i18n is set up; FastAPI with modular domain structure (auth, users, content, tests, analytics, etc.), PostgreSQL/SQLAlchemy asyncpg, Redis caching, MinIO storage, JWT auth with RBAC middleware, and Docker Compose orchestration are all in place. Services start and respond correctly per handoff verification. However, the commit only contains .factory/ infrastructure files; the actual scaffold files are uncommitted. Lingering issues include orphaned Go files in the Python codebase (9 .go files in apps/api/internal/) which are known but not addressed by this feature. No blocking code-level issues found in the scaffold itself.
